text.title: Terse Default Tool Output Procedure
text.summary: 4-step procedure to retrofit an existing list/search tool to terse-default + verbosity opt-in.

step.name: measure-worst-case
step.action: Run 20 representative calls capturing response token-count. If p95 > 500 tokens, this methodology applies.
step.decision-gate: If p95 < 500 tokens, skip — overhead exceeds the saving.

step.name: design-summary-shape
step.input: List of fields the agent actually consumes downstream (audit trace if available)
step.action: Pick the smallest subset that lets the agent decide its next action: always include the primary key, one ranking/recency hint, and one one-line headline. Drop everything else.
step.output: Summary shape spec (column list, ~30 tokens per row target)

step.name: implement-format-knob
step.action: Add a `format: Literal["summary","full"]` parameter defaulting to `"summary"`. Add `truncated` + `total_hits` to every response. For `full` mode, require a `reason: str` argument.
step.output: Modified tool returning the envelope from `02-output-contract.xml`

step.name: wire-eval-gate
step.input: Tool deployed to staging + agent trace store
step.action: Add a recurring eval: per-task ratio of `format="full"` calls. Alert if > 30% without a logged justification. Validate every response against the schema in CI.
step.output: Dashboard + alert wired; tool ready for production agents.
step.decision-gate: If raw-mode rate stays > 30% even with justifications, summary shape is missing required fields — return to step 2.
